name: libimobiledevice
summary: A library to communicate with services on iOS devices using native protocols.

documentation.readme: A library to communicate with services on iOS devices using native protocols.
usage_sections: Usage: Documentation about using the library in your application is not available yet. The "hacker way" for now is to look at the implementation of the included utilities.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the iOS device communication library category. Fits when the engagement needs: A library to communicate with services on iOS devices using native protocols.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A library to communicate with services on iOS devices using native protocols.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/libimobiledevice/libimobiledevice.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `./autogen.sh`. Further invocations from upstream: `./autogen.sh --prefix=/opt/local --enable-debug`; `./autogen.sh --with-gnutls`. A library to communicate with services on iOS devices using native protocols.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases, and licence before use. Operates against the operator's own authorized scope only.

use_cases: When you need iOS device communication library, use libimobiledevice because A library to communicate with services on iOS devices using native protocols.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./autogen.sh` as documented upstream for libimobiledevice.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./autogen.sh --prefix=/opt/local --enable-debug` as documented upstream for libimobiledevice.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./autogen.sh --with-gnutls` as documented upstream for libimobiledevice.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./autogen.sh --with-mbedtls mbedtls_INCLUDES=/opt/local/include mbedtls_LIBDIR=/opt/local/lib` as documented upstream for libimobiledevice.
When chaining iOS device communication library tools, pair libimobiledevice with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libimobiledevice --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
